claim: Hematopoietic progenitor cells are never susceptible to HIV-1 infection ex vivo.

result.claim: Hematopoietic progenitor cells are never susceptible to HIV-1 infection ex vivo.
result.reasoning: The claim that hematopoietic progenitor cells are never susceptible to HIV-1 infection ex vivo is refuted by direct evidence. Paper 90e6d9bd1cf44368a9ea4159e434576b64beb9b6, "HIV-1 Infection of Long-Lived Hematopoietic Precursors In Vitro and In Vivo" (2022), explicitly states that "Low-level infection/transduction of HSPCs, including hematopoietic stem cells (HSCs) and multipotent progenitors (MPP), was observed" in in vitro infection experiments (Proposition ID: prop_83998). The abstract of this paper further confirms "susceptibility of several distinct HSPC subpopulations to HIV-1 infection in vitro" and provides "functional proof of susceptibility of primitive HSPCs to HIV-1 infection" through multi-lineage colony formation and repetitive replating of transduced cells. While the credibility rating for this paper is low (1.5/5), the evidence directly contradicts the "never susceptible" aspect of the claim.

evidence.text: Oxidized bases are observed in CD34+ hematopoietic progenitor cells of MDS patients.
evidence.source: Oxidative Stress and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Myelodysplastic Syndrome: Roles in Development, Diagnosis, Prognosis, and Treatment.

evidence.text: The CD34+ cells derived from virus-exposed cells exhibited decreased hematopoiesis ex vivo.
evidence.source: MicroRNA target homeobox messenger RNA in HIV induced hematopoietic inhibition.
